chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يَضْرِبُ الرَّجُلَ فَيَذْهَبُ سَمْعُهُ وَ بَصَرُهُ وَ عَقْلُهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – The man strikes the man, so his hearing, and his vision, and his intellect goes

text: قَضَى أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فِي رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ رَجُلًا بِعَصاً فَذَهَبَ سَمْعُهُ وَ بَصَرُهُ وَ لِسَانُهُ وَ عَقْلُهُ وَ فَرْجُهُ وَ انْقَطَعَ جِمَاعُهُ وَ هُوَ حَيٌّ بِسِتِّ دِيَاتٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Khalid Al Barqy, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Ibrahim Bin Umar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> judged regarding a man who struck a man with a stick, so his hearing, and his vision, and his tongue, and his intellect, and his private parts ceased to function and his (ability) to copulate went, but he was alive - (a judgement of) six wergilds’.
